patch.value: Automate conditional daily releases with lossless pm changelog generation

Adopt pm-cli's daily conditional release model: skip when no publishable changes exist after the latest tag, generate the full changelog from repo-local pm data, prepare a calendar version, verify, push a release commit and tag, publish once to npm with provenance, and prove both npx and bunx consumption.

Every pre-existing CHANGELOG.md statement is represented by closed pm lineage and remains visible after full-history generation; Scheduled and manual auto-release skips cleanly when no publishable changes exist since the latest tag; A new tag drives one provenance-backed npm publication and verifies both npx and bunx launch paths
Remove manual release drift without losing historical release knowledge.
Makes releases repeatable, auditable, and consumable by both Node and Bun users.
A documented, tested conditional release pipeline backed by pm-changelog.
The repository has unreleased changes and only a manual publish workflow; its March history predates repo-local pm tracking.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: The legacy changelog is a migration source of truth and must not be overwritten until every statement has tracker-backed coverage.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Bun consumes packages from the npm registry; publish exactly once to npm, then verify Bun installation and bunx execution instead of attempting a duplicate registry publish.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: pm-changelog full-history mode assigns explicitly released historical items to their release windows even when those items are backfilled later.
